Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ‘Carrizo’ citrange; ‘Duncan’ grapefruit; ‘Hudson’ grapefruit; ‘Ora’ mandarin × ‘Hudson’ grapefruit F1 progeny
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 2× CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) for Cas9; shortened Arabidopsis U6 promoter (Pol III) for sgRNA expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in neohesperidoside-producing grapefruit and segregating F1 progeny; absent from non-producing ‘Ora’ mandarin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavanone-7-O-glucosides / flavanone glycoside acceptors leading to neohesperidosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of bitter flavanone-neohesperidosides by transferring rhamnose in a 1,2 linkage to flavanone glycosides, directing flux away from rutinosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product; branch-switch enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavanone-neohesperidosides (naringin, neohesperidin, poncirin); flavanone-rutinosides (narirutin, hesperidin, didymin)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids are health-benefitting compounds; neohesperidosides confer bitterness; flavanone-neohesperidosides have been implicated in antifungal defense, though physiological roles in citrus remain unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Presence of 1,2RhaT correlates with bitter neohesperidoside accumulation; CRISPR/Cas9 inactivation abolishes naringin, neohesperidin, and poncirin biosynthesis and concomitantly increases tasteless rutinosides narirutin, hesperidin, and didymin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Segregation analysis in ‘Ora’ mandarin × ‘Hudson’ grapefruit F1 population; diagnostic PCR; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout/editing in ‘Carrizo’ citrange and ‘Duncan’ grapefruit; Sanger sequencing of edited alleles; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown in net house or tissue culture; metabolite analysis in young leaves; no specific environmental treatment central to metabolic interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling by LC–MS; targeted genomic PCR and Sanger sequencing; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible unknown consequences from loss of flavanone-neohesperidosides, which have been implicated in antifungal activity and stress inducibility; exact physiological roles remain unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Genome editing of 1,2RhaT can generate non-bitter or less bitter grapefruit and trifoliate-derived citrus while maintaining overall flavonoid content; useful for breeding cold-hardy, HLB-tolerant, more palatable citrus and expanding consumer acceptance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The gene occurs as a single gene in citrus species according to segregation in the F1 population; mutations were 1-bp insertions causing premature stop codons and truncated proteins; edited plants in this study were transgenic GMO lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 1,2RhaT occurrence fully cosegregated with neohesperidoside production in an ‘Ora’ mandarin × ‘Hudson’ grapefruit population, supporting it as a single citrus gene linked to bitter flavanone-neohesperidoside biosynthesis. 2. CRISPR/Cas9-induced 1-bp insertions in 1,2RhaT in ‘Carrizo’ citrange and ‘Duncan’ grapefruit created truncated proteins and completely abolished bitter naringin, neohesperidin, and poncirin accumulation. 3. Loss of 1,2RhaT redirected metabolic flux toward tasteless flavanone-rutinosides, markedly increasing narirutin, hesperidin, and didymin while conserving overall flavanone levels.
